title: Famous Canvases
editorialSummary: Paintings grouped by the artist who made them. False trail: Starry Night, The Night Watch, and Girl with Pearl Earring are all world-famous paintings that players may try to group together as 'most famous artworks ever' — but the correct grouping is by painter. 'Vitruvian Man' is technically a drawing not a painting, but it is among da Vinci's most attributed works. 'The Anatomy Lesson' refers specifically to 'The Anatomy Lesson of Dr. Nicolaes Tulp' by Rembrandt (1632). 'Jewish Bride' is the common name for Rembrandt's 'The Jewish Bride' (c.1666).

meta.gptRationale: Most works and painters are very famous, so only a few less ubiquitous titles create resistance.
